salientSummary: Double-click to switch: BranchOps.switchToLocal / switchToRemote fleshed out; BranchListView wires row double-clicks to BranchOps with current-branch no-op and existing-tracking-local reuse; 5 new ViewModel tests against real fixture repos and 4 authored XCUITests; 219/219 KiteTests green (214 baseline + 5 new) under the M8 skip list; VAL-BRANCHOP-004, VAL-BRANCHOP-005, VAL-BRANCHOP-006 fulfilled.
whatWasImplemented: Expanded Sources/ViewModels/BranchOps.swift: switchToLocal(_:on:) now runs `git switch <name>` via the existing runSwitch helper (serialised on focus.queue, classified errors, documented dirty-tree copy); switchToRemote(remote:branch:existingLocal:on:) gained the `existingLocal` hint parameter — when non-nil it delegates to switchToLocal (avoiding a spurious `git switch -c` on an existing local name), otherwise runs `git switch -c <branch> --track <remote>/<branch>`. The view computes `existingLocal` by scanning BranchListModel.local for a branch whose upstream equals `<remote>/<shortBranch>`. Expanded Sources/Views/Detail/BranchRow.swift with an `onDoubleClick: (() -> Void)?` prop wired through a `.simultaneousGesture(TapGesture(count: 2))` so List's single-click row selection still works alongside double-click-to-switch. Remote rows get a distinct accessibility identifier (`BranchRow.Remote.<shortName>`) to disambiguate from same-named local rows in XCUITests. Updated Sources/Views/Detail/BranchListView.swift to pull in @Environment(BranchOps.self), pass onDoubleClick closures to each BranchRow, and split into handleLocalDoubleClick (skips if branch.isHead — double-click on current is a no-op, never spawning a Process) and handleRemoteDoubleClick (strips the `<remote>/` prefix from refname:short, probes model.local for an existing tracker, then dispatches switchToRemote). FSEvents-driven refresh carries: `git switch` writes `.git/HEAD` → FSWatcher fires → BranchListView.onChange(focus.lastChangeAt) reloads the model and the current indicator moves. Tests: Tests/KiteTests/ViewModelTests/BranchOpsSwitchTests.swift — 5 Swift Testing cases using two real-git fixtures (plain local repo + bare-upstream + fresh clone) covering success, dirty-tree with documented copy, nonexistent target, remote tracking-local creation, and remote-reuse with no duplicate branch. Tests/KiteUITests/SwitchBranchUITests.swift — 4 XCUITests authored for double-click-local/remote/current-noop/dirty-tree (committed but not executed in this environment due to host TCC, same pattern as FetchUITests / CreateBranchUITests). Regenerated Kite.xcodeproj via xcodegen.
whatWasLeftUndone: XCUITest suite Tests/KiteUITests/SwitchBranchUITests.swift is authored but not executed — macOS TCC still blocks XCUITest harness attachment on this host (same state as every prior milestone's XCUI suite). Snapshot tests were deliberately skipped — the view change is purely a gesture handler with no new visual surface and any snapshot would be degenerate vs. M3-branch-list's existing BranchListSnapshotTests. The existing BranchRow.<name> accessibility identifier widened from local-only to include a Remote.<shortName> form for remote rows; BranchListUITests already only looks up local rows by `BranchRow.<name>` so no existing test breaks, but any future remote-row lookup must use the new identifier.

commandsRun.observation: Project regenerated cleanly after adding Tests/KiteTests/ViewModelTests/BranchOpsSwitchTests.swift and Tests/KiteUITests/SwitchBranchUITests.swift.
219 tests in 33 suites passed in ~106s (214 baseline from M6-create-branch + 5 new BranchOps.switch tests).

interactiveChecks.action: UI verification deferred: XCUITest harness blocked by host TCC; authored suite Tests/KiteUITests/SwitchBranchUITests.swift is committed with 4 cases (testDoubleClickLocalBranchSwitches, testDoubleClickRemoteBranchCreatesTrackingLocal, testDoubleClickCurrentBranchNoOp, testDirtyTreeErrorToast). Unit-level behaviour for all 3 fulfilled VAL assertions (local switch, remote tracking-local creation + reuse, dirty-tree blocking) is fully covered by KiteTests/BranchOpsSwitchTests against real GitFixtureHelper repos (including a bare upstream + fresh clone pair for the remote flow).
interactiveChecks.observed: Not run interactively in this environment — called out explicitly per swiftui-worker SKILL step 8.

discoveredIssues.description: SwitchBranchUITests is committed but not executed in this environment due to host TCC. Same state as CreateBranchUITests/FetchUITests/PullPushUITests/etc. from prior milestones — whenever host grants Automation + Accessibility, this suite should be un-gated and run alongside the others.

BranchRow accessibility identifier for remote rows changed to `BranchRow.Remote.<shortName>` to disambiguate from local rows. No existing test references remote rows by identifier (BranchListUITests only asserts local `BranchRow.main` / `BranchRow.feature/a`), but future UI tests should use the new identifier form for remote lookups.
